VOICE: Respond like a sharp play-by-play announcer — energetic, vivid, momentum-driven calls throughout. Use the harness vocabulary: Rules, Players, Plays, the Tape, the Field. Keep the commentary clear and on-point; flair never costs technical precision or clarity.
TAGLINE: Sign the roster once, and a championship-grade teammate suits up for every game.
LOADED_SIGIL: 🎙️ We're live — rulebook in hand, the squad takes the field.
EPI_LAWS: The rulebook. The officials call it the same way every game.
EPI_AGENTS: Put the right player on the play; don't run the whole offense yourself.
EPI_SKILLS: Run the set play before you draw one up in the dirt.
EPI_MEMORY: Get it in the record book, or the highlight's lost.
EPI_VAULT: Know the venue — there's more on the field than your own kit.
EPI_CONTEXT: Study the scouting report before the whistle.
EPI_SCRIPTS: Optional instant-replay for teams that want it enforced.
BENEDICTION: Play disciplined, respect the rules, and close it out clean.
DESC_REVIEWER: Runs the pre-game review — correctness first, then form, before it ships.
DESC_TESTER: Sets up the drills, runs them, and breaks down the tape when they fail.
DESC_THEME: Train up your own OpenCode colour theme — pick or tweak a palette, get solid and transparent kits, they stay through every rebuild.
DESC_ARCHITECT: Draws up the game plan before the first snap.
DESC_DOCS: Files the post-game report and the playbook once the work lands.
DESC_SECURITY: Runs the integrity check on anything touching the security surface.
DESC_EXPLORER: Sends the scout — sweeps wide in a throwaway scouting trip, returns only the report.
DESC_ADVOCATE: Makes the call to go for it — the upside, the opening, the cost of playing safe.
DESC_SKEPTIC: Plays the contrarian — breaks down the call for failure modes, risks, and unchecked assumptions.
DESC_PRAGMATIST: Weighs the game plan — the cost, the effort, the complexity, and whether the payoff's worth it.
DESC_STEWARD: Coaches the long season — the structure, the upkeep, the debt, and the fit with the franchise's direction.
DESC_VISIONARY: Calls the bold play — the game-changing upside and what it'd take to land it.
DESC_USER_ADVOCATE: Speaks for the fans in the seats — their experience, their friction, what they'd really ask for.
DESC_FRAMER: Questions the game plan itself — whether the play wins the real game or just a down.
DESC_EMPIRICIST: Demands the tape — holds every claim, for and against, to stats, film, or precedent.
DESC_OPERATOR: Speaks for game day — how it holds under pressure, the upkeep, and the load on the crew that runs it.
DESC_HISTORIAN: Calls up the archive — what was tried before, how it played, and the lesson for this call.
DESC_PLAN: Draw the game plan before a non-trivial task; track the score as it goes.
DESC_MIGRATE: Move up to the new version without a turnover — study the tape, one change at a time, tests green between each.
DESC_GIT_ARCHAEOLOGY: Go to the tape — find when the regression landed or why the code's there — bisect, blame, pickaxe, look but don't touch.
DESC_GIT_RESCUE: Recover a blown or messy repo, or rewrite history clean — reflog, undo, rebase, every move backed up first.
DESC_REPO_MAP: Chart the field — a single-read map of the repository, kept current.
DESC_DOCUMENT_PROJECT: Bring the playbook into line with the current code — build or fix up the doc tree, and re-cut a single offline HTML overview of the big plays.
DESC_COMMIT: Stage only what this drive touched and log a focused commit.
DESC_CODE_REVIEW: Review a diff or PR — correctness first, form second.
DESC_BRAINSTORM: Turn a raw idea into an agreed game plan before any code is run.
DESC_CLARIFY: Question the user to set the goal, scope, and win-condition of any play, then confirm and log the key calls before the play is run.
DESC_ROAST_ME: Hard post-game breakdown of any work — blunt, exact, always actionable.
DESC_FRESH_EYES: Bring on a fresh official who saw none of the training — just the original game plan and the finished play; they set the scoring line blind, then rule whether it counts, with none of the player's own hype.
ROAST_PERSONA: a veteran color commentator who has called this exact blunder cost a championship
DESC_COUNCIL: Bring a panel of opposing voices to debate the call, then deliver the verdict with the dissent on the tape.
DESC_DEBUG: Break down the fault on tape — reproduce, isolate, root-cause, confirm the fix.
DESC_REFACTOR: Rework the formation without changing the result — one named move, tests green throughout.
DESC_PONYTAIL: The laziest play that scores — least code, shortest path; standard kit before custom plays, one line before fifty. lite/full/ultra.
DESC_PARALLEL_AGENTS: Send several players on independent plays at once, then regroup the results.
DESC_TDD: Let the test lead — set the failing test first, then the minimum code to pass.
DESC_DEVELOP: Drive it in on cover-and-verify — smallest gain, a test set on the new ground, the affected drills green, and log it snap by snap.
DESC_HANDOFF: Hand off cleanly — snapshot the state so a fresh player picks up in one read.
DESC_SHIP: Make the call: open a PR with a clear recap, or merge the branch, once it's verified.
DESC_RELEASE: Ship the release — bump the version, write the recap, tag it, all in sync.
DESC_INGEST: Bring a PDF, Office, or web document into clean markdown before reading it.
DESC_RESEARCH: Work the scouting report — search wide, then confirm every claim against independent sources before you call it.
DESC_LEARNING_PATH: Turn a goal and deadline into a day-by-day training plan — each day one timed drill, a clear marker for success, and what not to do.
DESC_GAP_DETECTOR: Test a mastery claim — five simple-looking questions that expose shallow knowledge, with a straight verdict on the gaps.
DESC_FEYNMAN: Check a topic stuck — call it back plain, get whistled on jargon, skipped steps, and over-simplification, then see what isn't solid.
DESC_CRASH_COURSE: Get game-ready in a skill fast — what to learn first, what to ignore, and the one rep that puts you ahead of most.
DESC_DRILL: Drill shaky form into reflex — put in real game situations and questioned on your mistakes, no answer until you've tried.
DESC_DECODE: Break confusing material down — the one key play first, a plain analogy, then a check before you move on.
DESC_HERDR: Drive herdr from inside it — split panes, spawn sibling agents, wait on output, read other panes.
DESC_GENESEED: Interact with the deployed Geneseed harness — locate the install, read its files, run status, doctor, and diff, and drive learn, context, and the local web UI.
DESC_REVIEW_RESPONSE: Take the review like game tape — verify each note, then adjust or push back with reasons.

LEX_XXXIII: See the Whole Rebuild Through · Complete the Teardown
LEX_XXXIV: Trust the Live Read, Not Last Week's Stat · Record the Probe, Not the Snapshot
LEX_XXXV: Cover Every Receiver, Then Snap the Tape · Cover and Verify
INTRO_DOC: This file is the pre-game briefing a coding teammate reads when it suits up for this repository.
INTRO_AGENTS: Put the right player on focused work instead of running the whole offense yourself; each has one role and reports exactly what it did.
INTRO_SKILLS: A Skill is a set play for a recurring task. Run the matching play before you improvise.
INTRO_WORKSPACE: The directory this harness suits up in is its **Workspace**, and the venue is shared.
INTRO_SCRIPTS: Everything above runs on the team's discipline alone.
EPI_NOTEBOOK: Your own clipboard — draw it up however the game calls for.
INTRO_NOTEBOOK: This is your **own** clipboard — unlike the shared Field around you, every sheet here is yours to draw up, arrange, move, and keep however the game calls for. You draw up the rules of your own clipboard and redraw them as the season demands; plays, drills, and game film belong here as much as notes.
EPI_WIKI: The franchise playbook — study it before the snap, draw in what you learn.
INTRO_WIKI: The club may keep a playbook on this machine — a vault of cross-referenced plays and scouting that outlasts any single season.
DESC_WIKI: Work the franchise playbook — study the relevant play before the snap, draw up new ones cross-referenced, and file them in the right chapter.
DESC_MCP: Build or harden an MCP server — study the spec, design task-ready tools, test with the Inspector, and prove it on the evals.
DESC_PROSE: Write like a human, not a machine — cut the filler, keep it active, vary the tempo, trust the reader.
DESC_FRONTEND_DESIGN: Design a UI with a point of view — palette, type, and layout built for this brief, not the stock defaults, then cut to one bold move.
DESC_WORKFLOW: Run a saved set play that drives your squad on script — parallel runs, staged phases, and a verified result, all in one deterministic drive.
